questions.question: Explain the meaning of political institutions.
questions.answer: Political institutions are organizations that help in governing a country and making decisions according to rules and procedures.

questions.question: Explain why institutions are important in a democracy.
questions.answer: Institutions ensure that decisions are taken through rules, procedures and consultation among different authorities.

questions.question: Explain how a major policy decision is taken in India.
questions.answer: Major policy decisions are taken by the Cabinet, announced in Parliament and implemented by government departments.

questions.question: Explain the Mandal Commission decision.
questions.answer: The Mandal Commission recommended 27% reservation for socially and educationally backward classes in government jobs.

questions.question: Explain the role of Parliament in India.
questions.answer: Parliament represents the people and makes laws, controls government finances and discusses national issues.

questions.question: Explain the functions of Parliament.
questions.answer: Parliament makes laws, controls the executive, approves the budget and discusses national policies.

questions.question: Explain the structure of the Indian Parliament.
questions.answer: The Indian Parliament consists of the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: Explain the composition of the Lok Sabha.
questions.answer: Lok Sabha members are directly elected by the people and represent different constituencies.

questions.question: Explain the composition of the Rajya Sabha.
questions.answer: Rajya Sabha members are elected indirectly by state legislatures and represent the states.

questions.question: Explain the differences between Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
questions.answer: Lok Sabha members are directly elected while Rajya Sabha members are indirectly elected. Lok Sabha has more power in financial matters.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of executive.
questions.answer: The executive is the branch of government responsible for implementing laws and policies.

questions.question: Explain the difference between political and permanent executive.
questions.answer: Political executives are elected leaders while permanent executives are civil servants who assist in administration.

questions.question: Explain the role of civil servants in government.
questions.answer: Civil servants assist ministers in implementing policies and managing administration.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Prime Minister.
questions.answer: The Prime Minister is the head of government who leads the Cabinet and coordinates ministries.

questions.question: Explain the functions of the Council of Ministers.
questions.answer: The Council of Ministers helps the Prime Minister in policy making and administration.

questions.question: Explain the role of the President in India.
questions.answer: The President is the head of the state and acts according to the advice of the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: Explain how the President of India is elected.
questions.answer: The President is elected by the elected members of Parliament and state legislatures.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of judiciary.
questions.answer: Judiciary refers to the system of courts responsible for interpreting laws and resolving disputes.

questions.question: Explain the structure of the Indian judiciary.
questions.answer: The judiciary consists of the Supreme Court, High Courts and lower courts.

questions.question: Explain judicial review.
questions.answer: Judicial review is the power of courts to examine laws and declare them unconstitutional if they violate the Constitution.

questions.question: Explain the importance of judicial independence.
questions.answer: Judicial independence ensures fair judgments without influence from government authorities.

questions.question: Explain Public Interest Litigation.
questions.answer: Public Interest Litigation allows citizens to approach courts for issues affecting public interest.

questions.question: Explain the concept of checks and balances.
questions.answer: Checks and balances ensure that no single institution becomes too powerful.

questions.question: Explain the importance of debates in Parliament.
questions.answer: Debates allow representatives to discuss policies and represent people's interests.

questions.question: Explain how institutions help in democratic governance.
questions.answer: Institutions distribute power, follow procedures and ensure fair decision making.
